reasoning_content: We need to determine if 389 is a prime number. A prime number is greater than 1 and has no positive divisors other than 1 and itself. Check divisibility by small primes: 2: no, odd; 3: sum of digits 3+8+9=20, not divisible by 3; 5: does not end in 0 or 5; 7: 7*55=385, remainder 4, not divisible; 11: alternating sum 3-8+9=4, not divisible; 13: 13*29=377, remainder 12; 13*30=390, too high; 17: 17*22=374, remainder 15; 17*23=391, too high; 19: 19*20=380, remainder 9; 19*21=399, too high; 23: 23*16=368, remainder 21; 23*17=391, too high; 29: 29*13=377, remainder 12; 29*14=406, too high; 31: 31*12=372, remainder 17; 31*13=403, too high; 37: 37*10=370, remainder 19; 37*11=407, too high; sqrt(389) ~ 19.7, so we only need to test primes up to 19. Actually we tested up to 19, none divide. Also test 2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19. So 389 is prime. Answer yes.
